title: cyclosorus exindusiatus
intro: Cyclosorus exindusiatus, also known as the Chinese Leopards' Bane or Leopards' Bane, is a type of fern plant native to Asia, particularly in the tropical regions of China, Japan, and India. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Cyclosorus exindusiatus include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiviral agent and an antidote for snake bites. Herbal preparations of Cyclosorus exindusiatus include teas, infusions, and decoctions made from its rhizomes and leaves, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote health and well-being.
